skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with intentional aesthetics, high craft, and non-generic visual identity.
skill.summary: Design memorable, production-ready web interfaces with intentional aesthetics using the DFII framework for evaluating design decisions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 36 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation describing a frontend design methodology (DFII framework). No executable code, network calls, or command execution present. The LICENSE.txt is standard Apache 2.0 text. Safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Generic AI-generated UIs lack visual identity and memorability. This skill applies the DFII framework to create intentional, high-craft interfaces with cohesive aesthetics that stand out.
actual_capabilities: Apply the DFII framework to evaluate design direction impact and feasibility
Generate production-ready HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with intentional aesthetics
Select expressive typography paired with restrained body fonts
Create cohesive color systems using CSS variables
Implement purposeful motion and spatial composition
Ensure accessibility compliance including contrast, focus states, and keyboard navigation
limitations: Requires clear design intent input from the user
Output complexity matches design ambition - maximalist designs require more code
Does not integrate with backend systems or APIs
Focuses on visual design execution, not user research or testing

use_cases.title: Landing Page Design
use_cases.description: Create a memorable landing page with editorial brutalism aesthetic, including custom typography hierarchy, asymmetric layout, and high-impact entrance animations.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founder launching a new product

use_cases.title: Dashboard Component Library
use_cases.description: Build a cohesive set of dashboard components with luxury minimal aesthetic, using restrained color palette, precise spacing, and refined micro-interactions.
use_cases.target_user: SaaS developer building an analytics platform

use_cases.title: Brand Website Redesign
use_cases.description: Redesign an existing website with retro-futurist aesthetic direction, implementing custom gradient meshes, layered translucency, and distinctive motion patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Creative agency updating a client's digital presence

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Styling
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a signup form component using luxury minimal aesthetic. Include intentional typography choices, a dominant color story with one accent, and purposeful whitespace. Provide complete HTML and CSS.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a polished, conversion-focused form that stands out from generic designs.

prompt_templates.title: Full Page Design with DFII Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product landing page. First, evaluate the design direction using the DFII framework across all five dimensions. Target a score of 12 or higher. Use industrial utilitarian aesthetic with intentional asymmetric composition.
prompt_templates.scenario: Launching a developer tool and need a distinctive, memorable landing page.

prompt_templates.title: Design System Definition
prompt_templates.prompt: Define a complete design system for a fintech dashboard. Specify: display and body font pairings with rationale, CSS variable color system with one dominant tone and one accent, spacing rhythm, and motion philosophy. Then implement a sample card component.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a new product and need foundational design decisions before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Aesthetic Transformation
prompt_templates.prompt: Transform this generic button component into something memorable using editorial brutalism aesthetic. Explain what makes it distinctive from typical AI-generated UI. Include hover states and focus styles for accessibility.
prompt_templates.scenario: Refactoring existing generic components to have intentional visual identity.

output_examples.input: Design a hero section with retro-futurist aesthetic for a space tourism startup
output_examples.output: Hero section featuring: gradient mesh background with deep purple to teal transition, custom display font (Orbitron) for headline, asymmetric text layout with overlapping elements, grain overlay texture, single high-impact floating animation on CTA button, starfield particle effect using CSS only. DFII Score: 13 (Excellent execution with strong aesthetic cohesion).

output_examples.input: Create a pricing card component with luxury minimal aesthetic
output_examples.output: Three-tier pricing cards with: generous whitespace (48px base unit), refined serif display font (Playfair Display) paired with geometric sans (Jost), monochromatic base with gold accent only on primary CTA, subtle box-shadow with 20% opacity, smooth scale transform on hover, checkmark icons as SVG. Avoids generic purple gradients and symmetrical layouts.

best_practices: Always state the aesthetic direction explicitly before writing code
Use the DFII framework to validate design decisions achieve a score of 8 or higher
Include at least one memorable design anchor that differentiates from generic templates
anti_patterns: Using system fonts or AI defaults like Inter, Roboto, or Arial without intentional rationale
Applying generic purple-to-white SaaS gradients without aesthetic justification
Creating symmetrical, predictable section layouts that could be any template

faq.question: What is the DFII framework?
faq.answer: DFII (Design Feasibility and Impact Index) evaluates design directions across five dimensions: Aesthetic Impact, Context Fit, Implementation Feasibility, Performance Safety, and Consistency Risk. Scores range from -5 to +15, with 12+ indicating excellent design direction.

faq.question: Can this skill work with existing design systems?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your existing design tokens, font choices, or brand guidelines as context. The skill will apply the aesthetic methodology while respecting your constraints.

faq.question: Does this skill generate responsive designs?
faq.answer: Yes. All output includes responsive considerations. Specify target breakpoints if you have specific requirements, otherwise mobile-first responsive behavior is the default.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: Primary support for vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. React component output is available on request. CSS animations are preferred; Framer Motion is used only when complexity justifies it.

faq.question: How do I specify a different aesthetic direction?
faq.answer: State your desired aesthetic explicitly: brutalist, editorial, luxury minimal, retro-futurist, industrial, organic, playful, maximalist, or minimalist. You can combine two adjacent aesthetics but avoid blending more.

faq.question: Can this skill improve my existing components?
faq.answer: Yes. Share your current component code and specify the aesthetic transformation you want. The skill will refactor with intentional design decisions while maintaining functionality.
